methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: ILOPool.buy() called with raiseAmount and recipient
Whitelist check on recipient via _isWhitelisted
Hard cap check: saleInfo.hardCap - totalRaised >= raiseAmount
totalRaised incremented, totalSold() checked against maxSaleAmount
Mint new NFT or retrieve existing tokenId for recipient
Per-user cap check: raiseAmount <= maxCapPerUser - _position.raiseAmount
Liquidity calculation via LiquidityAmounts.getLiquidityForAmount0/1
Liquidity scaled by _vestingConfigs[0].shares / BPS
Position state updated (raiseAmount, liquidity, totalLiquidity)
RAISE_TOKEN transferred from msg.sender to ILOPool
transformations: liquidityDelta scaled by investor shares: liquidityDelta * shares / BPS (floor division)
Position vesting totalLiquidity set to cumulative position.liquidity
data_flows.trust_changes: msg.sender trusted as payer, recipient validated via whitelist only
exposure_risks: msg.sender != recipient allows third-party purchases on behalf of whitelisted users
manipulation_risks: Griefing: attacker fills a whitelisted user's cap by buying on their behalf without consent
Rounding loss: individual liquidityDelta calculations via floor division systematically undercount total liquidity vs launch-time calculation

data_flows.source: ILOManager.launch() called by anyone after launchTime
path: ILOManager.launch() validates launchTime, pool price, and pool count
ILOPool.launch() called via OnlyManager modifier
Total liquidity calculated from totalRaised via _saleAmountNeeded
addLiquidity() mints position on Uniswap V3 pool
Project vesting NFTs minted with liquidity shares from total liquidity
_refundProject sends remaining SALE_TOKEN to project admin
_launchSucceeded set to true
transformations: totalLiquidity → per-project-config shares: liquidity * config.shares / BPS
Leftover SALE_TOKEN balance → project admin refund
data_flows.trust_changes: Permissionless launch after launchTime; manager is trusted intermediary
validation_points: Price slippage check in addLiquidity (RAISE_TOKEN side only)
exposure_risks: _launchSucceeded set AFTER _refundProject external call - potential reentrancy window
manipulation_risks: SALE_TOKEN side of addLiquidity has amount0Min/amount1Min = 0, allowing value extraction if pool price shifts
Sum of individual investor liquidities ≠ total launch liquidity * investorShares / BPS due to accumulated rounding errors

path: Verify _launchSucceeded
Calculate claimable liquidity via _claimableLiquidity (vesting schedule)
pool.burn(TICK_LOWER, TICK_UPPER, liquidity2Claim) removes liquidity from shared Uniswap position
Read feeGrowthInside from Uniswap pool position
Calculate user's fee share using their positionLiquidity
Deduct platform fee from burn amounts, performance fee from trading fees
pool.collect(type(uint128).max) sweeps ALL owed tokens from the shared position
Transfer amount0/amount1 to token owner
Transfer amountCollected - amount to FEE_TAKER
transformations: Fee taker receives: amountCollected - userAmount (includes OTHER users' uncollected fees)
data_flows.trust_changes: FEE_TAKER receives tokens belonging to non-claiming users
exposure_risks: CRITICAL: collect(type(uint128).max) sweeps ALL owed tokens for the entire shared Uniswap position, not just the claiming user's portion
Other users' accumulated trading fees are sent to FEE_TAKER instead of being preserved
manipulation_risks: First claimer steals proportionally more fees from other users - incentive to claim first
Fee taker receives windfall from other users' uncollected fees

path: Transfer triggers _beforeTokenTransfer
If _whitelistContract != address(0), calls checkWhitelist(from, to, amount)
Checks if from == _pool and to != owner (buy from pool)
If locked, revert
Check blacklist, whitelist index, allowed index
Query oracle for ETH estimate: IOracle(_oracle).peek(amount)
Check _contributed[to] + estimatedETH <= _maxAddressCap
Update _contributed[to]
transformations: VULT amount → estimated ETH via UniswapV3Oracle.peek()
Oracle: TWAP tick → quotedWETHAmount → scaled by baseAmount * 95 / 1e20
sinks: _contributed mapping updated with estimated ETH value
data_flows.trust_changes: Oracle trusted for price estimation; TWAP integrity depends on pool observation history
exposure_risks: Oracle returns stale/manipulable price if observation history is short
If oracle pool just created, getOldestObservationSecondsAgo returns 0, period=0, consult reverts - DoS on all transfers
manipulation_risks: TWAP manipulation with sustained capital over 30 minutes to lower price estimate and bypass cap
If longestPeriod < PERIOD, TWAP window shortens to spot-like price, easier to manipulate

data_flows.source: Token holder or project admin initiating refund
path: claimRefund(tokenId): verify refund conditions (not launched, past deadline)
Set _refundTriggered on first call
Read position.raiseAmount, delete position data, burn NFT
Transfer RAISE_TOKEN to token owner
claimProjectRefund: transfer all SALE_TOKEN balance to project admin
transformations: Position deletion → RAISE_TOKEN refund at recorded raiseAmount
data_flows.trust_changes: Once refund triggered, launch permanently blocked
exposure_risks: Refund amount is the original raiseAmount, not current token balance - assumes sufficient balance
manipulation_risks: If RAISE_TOKEN is fee-on-transfer, actual balance < sum of raiseAmounts, last refunder cannot claim

core_rules: Launch and refund are mutually exclusive states (_launchSucceeded vs _refundTriggered)
Total vesting config shares must equal 10000 BPS exactly
Each vest schedule's shares must equal 10000 BPS exactly
First vesting config (index 0) is for investors (recipient=address(0)), rest for project
Sale token amount needed is calculated from raise amount at fixed price (SQRT_RATIO_X96)
Hard cap limits total raise, per-user cap limits individual contribution
Soft cap must be met for launch to succeed
Uniswap V3 pool price must match initialPoolPriceX96 at launch time
economic_incentives: Platform fee (BPS) deducted from claimed liquidity amounts
Performance fee (BPS) deducted from accumulated trading fees
Investors receive vested liquidity positions that unlock linearly over schedule
Project recipients receive liquidity shares that vest on separate schedules
governance_mechanisms: ILOManager owner sets fees, refund deadlines, pool implementation
Project admin creates pools, manages whitelist, transfers admin role
Refund deadline can be changed by ILOManager owner at any time (no timelock)
Platform/performance fees are snapshot at project creation but fee taker address is read dynamically

edge_cases.scenario: collect(type(uint128).max) in claim() sweeps ALL owed tokens from the shared Uniswap V3 position, including fees belonging to other users who haven't claimed yet
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Each user should receive only their proportional share of trading fees; unclaimed fees should remain available for other users
edge_cases.actual_behavior: First claimer's collect() sweeps all accumulated fees. Their calculated fee portion goes to user, the remainder (other users' fees) goes to FEE_TAKER. Subsequent claimers receive zero historical fees.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: FEE_TAKER systematically receives fees belonging to all non-claiming users. Race condition incentivizes early claims. Over time, significant value is siphoned from users to fee taker. Protocol could intentionally exploit this by being the first to claim a project vesting position.

edge_cases.scenario: Missing check that initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96 in ILOManager.initILOPool
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Price range should fully contain the initial price for both RAISE and SALE tokens to be required
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only checks sqrtRatioLowerX96 < initialPoolPriceX96. If price >= upper bound, liquidity calculations produce incorrect results - getLiquidityForAmount0/1 would use price outside the intended range
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Project admin sets tick range where price is at or above upper tick. This breaks the raise-to-liquidity math, potentially allowing purchase of positions at wrong prices or causing launch to fail unexpectedly.

edge_cases.scenario: Overflow in _unlockedLiquidity: vest.shares * totalLiquidity computed as uint128
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Safe multiplication with proper widening to uint256 before potential overflow
edge_cases.actual_behavior: vest.shares (uint16, max 10000) * totalLiquidity (uint128) is computed in uint128 arithmetic in Solidity 0.7.6. If totalLiquidity > 2^128 / 10000 ≈ 3.4e34, this silently overflows, producing wrong unlocked amount
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: For very large liquidity positions, overflow causes incorrect vesting calculations, potentially unlocking more or less liquidity than intended. While extreme values are needed, the overflow is silent in Solidity 0.7.6.

edge_cases.scenario: Liquidity accounting mismatch between individual buy() calculations and aggregate launch() calculation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Sum of individual position liquidities should equal the total position liquidity allocated for investors
edge_cases.actual_behavior: buy() calculates per-user: getLiquidity(raiseAmount_i) * shares / BPS (floor division each step). launch() calculates total: getLiquidity(totalRaised) * shares / BPS. Due to non-linearity of getLiquidity and accumulated rounding, sum(individual) ≠ total. This means some liquidity in Uniswap has no claimant, or claims exceed available.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If sum of tracked liquidities exceeds actual Uniswap position liquidity, late claimers' burn() calls will revert. Locked liquidity represents permanent value loss.

edge_cases.scenario: Vest schedule with start == end (zero duration)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should be fully unlocked at start time or rejected during validation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _validateVestSchedule does not check start < end. In _unlockedLiquidity, when block.timestamp == start == end, the else branch executes with denominator (end - start) * BPS = 0, causing FullMath.mulDiv to revert on division by zero. Claims are permanently DOSed.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious project admin creates pool with zero-duration vest, permanently locking investor funds since claim() always reverts.

edge_cases.scenario: UniswapV3Oracle.peek() with newly created pool (insufficient observation history)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Oracle should gracefully handle pools with short history
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If getOldestObservationSecondsAgo returns 0, period becomes 0, OracleLibrary.consult reverts with 'BP'. This DoSes all token transfers through VultisigWhitelisted during the whitelist period.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If oracle is set before pool has any observation history, all buys from Uniswap pool are blocked. Could delay token distribution.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Contract should track actual received amounts
edge_cases.actual_behavior: buy() records raiseAmount as deposited but actual received amount is less due to transfer fee. On refund, contract may not have sufficient balance to cover all refunds. Last refunder loses funds.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If RAISE_TOKEN charges transfer fees, the pool becomes underfunded. During refund, the last users cannot withdraw their full raiseAmount.

edge_cases.scenario: totalRaised exactly equals softCap but less than hardCap; raiseAmount = 0 passed to buy()
edge_cases.actual_behavior: raiseAmount=0 passes the HC check and UC check. getLiquidity returns 0, which is caught by require(liquidityDelta > 0, 'ZA'). But if _vestingConfigs[0].shares < BPS, a non-zero liquidityDelta could become 0 after mulDiv, also caught. So zero buys are prevented.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Minimal - the ZA check handles this.

edge_cases.scenario: ILOManager owner changes refund deadline to past timestamp while ILO is ongoing
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should have safeguards to prevent premature refund
edge_cases.actual_behavior: setRefundDeadlineForProject has no constraints. Owner can set refundDeadline to 0, immediately enabling refund via the refundable() modifier, even during active sale.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious or compromised ILOManager owner can trigger premature refund, disrupting active ILO sales and preventing launch even if demand exists.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fees should be bounded to reasonable limits
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No upper bound check on PLATFORM_FEE or PERFORMANCE_FEE. If set to 10000 BPS, users receive 0 tokens on claim. All value goes to FEE_TAKER.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: ILOManager owner sets 100% fees after project initialization. Fees are snapshot at project creation in _cachedProject, so only affects new projects. But no bound validation exists.

edge_cases.scenario: Multiple buy() calls for same recipient accumulate liquidity but vesting totalLiquidity is overwritten each time
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _positionVests[tokenId].totalLiquidity = _position.liquidity, which IS cumulative since _position.liquidity += liquidityDelta. So totalLiquidity is correctly updated. No issue.

assumption_violations: Assumes single Uniswap V3 position's fees can be correctly distributed across multiple virtual sub-positions via collect(max) - this is INCORRECT
Assumes sum of individually calculated liquidities equals total calculated liquidity - violated by rounding
Assumes RAISE_TOKEN and SALE_TOKEN are standard ERC20 without transfer fees or rebasing
Assumes initialPoolPriceX96 is between SQRT_RATIO_LOWER_X96 and SQRT_RATIO_UPPER_X96 - only lower bound checked
Assumes vest schedule entries have start < end - not validated
Assumes oracle pool has sufficient observation history - not guaranteed
Assumes Uniswap V3 pool price cannot be manipulated between ILOManager.launch price check and ILOPool.launch addLiquidity
game_theory_risks: Race to claim first: earlier claimers capture a larger share of accumulated trading fees due to the collect(max) bug
FEE_TAKER benefits from users NOT claiming - more uncollected fees are swept to feeTaker when someone eventually claims
Project admin can manipulate whitelist to front-run their own purchases then remove whitelist
ILOManager owner has unilateral control over refund deadlines - can force refund or prevent it
Permissionless launch() allows anyone to trigger launch at launchTime - project admin cannot delay if conditions are unfavorable
integration_risks: Uniswap V3 pool.burn() + pool.collect() pattern assumes exclusive position ownership - violated by shared virtual positions
Uniswap V3 pool price check in ILOManager.launch() can be sandwiched if pool already has liquidity from other sources
External oracle dependency for whitelist cap enforcement - oracle failure blocks all transfers
Clones.cloneDeterministic uses fixed implementation - if implementation has bugs, all cloned pools are affected
ILOPool relies on MANAGER for project data - if MANAGER is upgraded or state corrupted, pools break
calculation_errors: In _unlockedLiquidity: vest.shares (uint16) * totalLiquidity (uint128) computed in uint128, overflow possible for large totalLiquidity values
In _unlockedLiquidity: (vest.end - vest.start) * BPS computed in uint64 space, theoretically overflowable for very long vest durations though practically unlikely
In buy(): liquidityDelta calculated per-user then scaled; sum of per-user values ≠ aggregate calculation due to floor division rounding
In claim(): fee calculation for individual user correct in isolation, but collect(max) collects fees for entire shared position - mathematical inconsistency

token_semantics.notes: VultisigWhitelisted._beforeTokenTransfer calls Whitelist.checkWhitelist on every transfer when whitelist contract is set. Owner can disable by setting whitelist to address(0). Fixed supply of 100M tokens. Has approveAndCall for arbitrary external contract calls.
Assumed standard ERC20. If fee_on_transfer or rebasing, refund accounting breaks (balance < sum of raiseAmounts). TransferHelper.safeTransferFrom used. Decimals unknown - depends on deployment.

Assumed standard ERC20. _refundProject uses balanceOf(address(this)) which is correct for standard tokens but would include unexpected amounts for rebasing tokens. Used as Uniswap V3 concentrated liquidity pairing.
Standard WETH. PeripheryPayments.pay handles ETH↔WETH wrapping. receive() restricted to WETH9 only.
ERC721 with enumerable. Each investor has at most 1 NFT per pool (checked via balanceOf(recipient)==0). Burned on refund. Position data stored separately in _positions mapping.

invariants.name: launch_refund_exclusivity
invariants.description: At most one of _launchSucceeded and _refundTriggered can be true. Both start false. Once one is set, the other is permanently blocked.

invariants.name: total_raised_consistency
invariants.description: totalRaised should equal sum of all position raiseAmounts. Maintained by buy() incrementing both. Broken if raiseAmount tracking is incorrect.

invariants.name: vesting_shares_total_BPS
invariants.description: Sum of all vesting config shares must equal 10000 BPS exactly. Validated in _validateSharesAndVests.

invariants.name: vest_schedule_shares_total_BPS
invariants.description: For each vesting config, sum of schedule shares must equal 10000 BPS. Validated in _validateVestSchedule.

invariants.name: position_liquidity_conservation_VIOLATED
invariants.description: INVARIANT VIOLATED: Sum of individual position liquidities (investors + project) may not equal actual Uniswap position liquidity due to rounding in per-user calculations vs aggregate calculation.

invariants.name: fee_distribution_fairness_VIOLATED
invariants.description: INVARIANT VIOLATED: collect(type(uint128).max) sweeps all owed tokens from shared Uniswap position. Fees belonging to non-claiming users are redirected to FEE_TAKER instead of being preserved.
